lesson: For shell-tools planning, inspect install.sh before adding roadmap items: it already writes omni-select, navmenu.py, zsh aliases, tmux, starship, and micro config, while index.sh exposes only a small subset. Prioritize surfacing and modularizing existing features before adding new tools.

Track AI CLI installers as a separate roadmap layer from base shell setup. Start with a doctor/status checker, then add per-tool installers and shared provider profiles so secrets stay outside repo history and setup remains opt-in.

Represent shared agent skills as a cross-CLI compatibility layer: Codex and Claude use SKILL.md directories, while Gemini CLI support should target extension templates with gemini-extension.json and GEMINI.md.

Shell enhancement implementation is cheapest when added to the generated zsh config in install.sh and committed in small TODO batches. Keep interactive functions in zsh when they must cd or edit BUFFER; standalone scripts are better only when shell state does not need to change.

For broad shell-tools roadmap implementation, one commit per TODO item keeps reviewable history. Index/TUI should be updated alongside each user-facing script, and scripts should be syntax-checked individually because Termux APIs can block or fail differently from ordinary Linux commands.
